title: Care coordination review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.
examination.rationale: The exact priorities section supports clinician-entered goals, treatment burden, service coordination, agreed responsibilities, questions, and follow-up without creating a care plan automatically.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — treatment burden, day-to-day life, carers, goals, values, and priorities

medication.text: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.
medication.rationale: The exact communication section supports documenting available diagnoses, medicines, changes, allergies, pending information, responsibilities, patient or carer involvement, and communication between services without changing care.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.

source_limitations: Conditions, services, responsible clinician, pending results, medicine changes, capacity, carer involvement, referral, appointment, escalation, and timeline remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
